intro: Collabium chapaense, also known as Vietnamese Orchid, is a type of orchid species found in Vietnam, characterized by its unique flowers and medicinal properties. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, inflammation, and respiratory problems, due to its potential health benefits such as antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial activities. Bioactive constituents of Collabium chapaense include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional Vietnamese medicine, the plant is often used to make tea, infusions, and decoctions, with the most common herbal preparations being teas, tinctures, and capsules, which are used to treat a range of health conditions.
